rulesets.name: LT-G: var-match equals operator
rulesets.note:
Tests operator: equals with value — used by production rules 'Upsert', 'New location', and 'Known location' to gate on LT-changed = true.

Click 'Seed LT-G'. Expected: Baseline (blue) + T22 (green) appear, T23 stays silent. If T22 does not appear, the equals operator is broken. If only Baseline appears but not T22, equals silently fails. If T23 (red) appears, something is very wrong.

rules.name: Keyword: set LT-changed = true on marker
Baseline: LT-changed is set (var-match set)
rules.note: If this fires but T22 does not, the keyword ran but equals is broken. If this does NOT fire, the keyword failed — not an equals operator issue.

rules.name: T22: var-match equals true — SHOULD fire
T23: var-match equals false — should NOT fire

actions.title: T23: UNEXPECTED — equals false fired
actions.message: LT-changed = '{{LT-changed}}' — matched 'false' when it should be 'true'

rulesets.name: LT-H: Parent vs sub-location extraction
rulesets.note:
Two seed badges, two bottom badges. Click a Seed to create a message, then click T25 and T26 on that message.

On 'Tavern - Kitchen': T25 → 'Tavern - Kitchen', T26 → 'Tavern' (stops before dash).
On 'Inn': T25 → 'Inn', T26 → 'Inn' (no dash, both patterns match to ]).

rules.name: T25: extract full location — badge reads {{message}}
rules.note: Same regex as production 'Extract full location'. Captures everything after 📍 up to ]. On Seed A should show 'Tavern - Kitchen'. On Seed B should show 'Inn'.

rules.name: T26: extract parent location — badge reads {{message}}
rules.note: Same regex as production 'Extract parent location'. Captures up to ' - ' or ] or end. On Seed A should show 'Tavern' only. On Seed B should show 'Inn'.

rulesets.name: LT-I: Chatvar persist loop
rulesets.note:
Tests the full two-turn loop: extract → persist chatvar → next turn condition sees prior value.

Step 1: Click 'Clear chatvar'.
Step 2: Click 'Seed: Inn' — T27 (green, change from empty) fires; click 'Read chatvar' — should show 'Inn'.
Step 3: Click 'Seed: Inn' again — T28 (orange, same loc) fires; T27 stays silent.
Step 4: Click 'Seed: Tavern' — T27 (green, change) fires; T28 stays silent.

rules.name: Persist: write LT-diag_loc to chatvar
rules.note: Mirrors production 'Persist current location to chatvar'. Fires every turn when 📍 was extracted. This is what makes the next turn's condition see the prior location.

rules.name: T27: location changed — chatvar != current
rules.note: Fires on first Seed Inn (chatvar empty) and Seed Tavern (chatvar=Inn). Same condition as production 'Detect location change'.

rules.name: T28: same location — chatvar = current
rules.note: Fires on second Seed Inn (chatvar already 'Inn'). Confirms the loop correctly sees no change.

rulesets.name: LT-J: Event → event cross-rule var timing
rulesets.note:
Tests whether an event rule's compose output is visible to a subsequent event rule's var-match trigger in the same turn. This is the exact pattern production uses: 'Detect location change' (event rule) sets LT-changed, then 'New/Known location' (event rules) gate on var-match LT-changed equals 'true'.

Click 'Seed LT-J'. Expected: Baseline (blue) + T29 (green) appear, T30 stays silent. If T29 does NOT appear but Baseline does, event→event var timing is broken — the engine pre-evaluates all triggers before running any actions.

rules.name: Rule A: event rule that sets LT-j-flag (mirrors 'Detect location change')
rules.note: Fires on every MESSAGE_RECEIVED. Sets LT-j-flag so Rule B can see it. No conditions — guaranteed to fire so we isolate the timing question.

rules.name: Baseline: LT-j-flag set (var-match set)
rules.note: Confirms Rule A ran. If Baseline fires but T29 does not, the variable exists but equals evaluation is broken in this context.

rules.name: T29: event→event timing — var-match equals sees Rule A output
rules.note: If this fires, the engine runs Rule A's actions before evaluating Rule B's triggers — sequential execution. Production chain works.

actions.title: T29: event→event timing [PASS]
actions.message: LT-j-flag set by event rule A is visible to event rule B's var-match

rules.name: T30: LT-j-flag not-set — should NOT fire
rules.note: If this fires, Rule A's compose was not visible when Rule B's trigger evaluated — engine batches trigger evaluation before running actions.

actions.title: T30: FAIL — event→event timing broken
actions.message: LT-j-flag was not visible to Rule B — engine pre-evaluates all triggers before running actions

rulesets.name: LT-K: compose-empty → set vs not-set vs empty
rulesets.note:
Proves that compose with an empty result writes '' to the var (making it 'set' to empty string), NOT leaving it unset. This is the root cause of the location-tracker bug: production used not-set/set to route new vs known locations, but compose always writes the var — so not-set never fires after 'Detect location change' runs.

Click 'Seed LT-K'. Expected: T31 (green, set fires) + T33 (green, empty fires). T32 (red, not-set) and T34 (red, not-empty) should stay silent.

rules.name: Keyword: compose LT-lb_entry to empty string (mirrors fuzzy no-match)
rules.note: Sets LT-lb_entry = '' — same result as {{fuzzy:80:candidates:NoMatch}} returning empty. Template is intentionally blank.

rules.name: T31: var-match set — SHOULD fire (compose wrote '', var exists)
rules.note: If this fires, compose with empty result still marks the var as 'set'. The production not-set gate was therefore wrong.

actions.title: T31: set fires [PASS — confirms bug]
actions.message: LT-lb_entry is 'set' even though compose wrote '' — not-set would never fire

rules.name: T32: var-match not-set — should NOT fire
rules.note: If this fires, compose with empty result leaves the var unset — the production ruleset was correct and the bug is elsewhere.

actions.title: T32: not-set fired [UNEXPECTED]
actions.message: compose '' left LT-lb_entry unset — production not-set gate would work after all

rules.name: T33: var-match empty — SHOULD fire (var is set to '')
rules.note: Confirms that 'empty' is the correct operator for routing new locations in production.

actions.title: T33: empty fires [PASS — correct fix]
actions.message: LT-lb_entry is empty string — 'empty' operator correctly identifies new-location case

rules.name: T34: var-match not-empty — should NOT fire
rules.note: Confirms 'not-empty' correctly identifies known locations (non-empty fuzzy match result).

actions.title: T34: not-empty fired [UNEXPECTED]
actions.message: LT-lb_entry should be empty but not-empty matched — compose wrote a non-empty value
